hook.text: The schema changed. New column, renamed table, dropped constraint. How do you apply it safely? Migrations.

buildup.text: A migration is a versioned script: 001_create_users.sql, 002_add_email.sql. Applied in order, tracked.

discovery.text: Tools like Flyway, Alembic, and Prisma Migrate run pending migrations and record which ones have been applied.

twist.text: Migrations are irreversible in production. You can't un-drop a column. Always add, then backfill, then remove.

climax.text: Every schema change goes through a migration. No manual ALTER TABLE in production. Ever.

punchline.text: Migrations: versioned, ordered, automated schema changes.

quiz.question: Why should you never run manual ALTER TABLE in production?
options: Manual changes aren't tracked, versioned, or repeatable across environments
ALTER TABLE doesn't work in production databases
Manual changes are always faster than migrations
